proposed_hypotheses.text: The overall objective_response rate in the cohort is approximately 15-25%, consistent with a hard-to-treat AML population.
Older feature_011 (age) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
feature_010 (likely sex) is associated with a difference in objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Overall objective_response = 0.1875 (9375/50000 responders). Hypothesis supported.
Univariate logistic of objective_response on feature_011 (age in years): beta=-0.00016/yr, OR=0.9998 per year, p=0.88. No measurable age effect.
Response rate 18.91% (feature_010=1) vs 18.62% (feature_010=0); chi2=0.65, p=0.42. No effect of feature_010.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal variable feature_004 (likely cytogenetic-risk or ECOG performance status) are associated with monotonically lower objective_response rate.
Higher feature_020 (continuous, range 0-23.6, plausibly WBC or LDH) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher feature_024 (continuous, range 20-100) is associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: feature_004 levels 0/1/2 have response rates 23.10% / 17.43% / 12.91% respectively (chi2=413.3, df=2, p=1.8e-90). Ordinal logistic beta = -0.40, OR=0.67 per level, p<1e-90.
feature_020 OR per SD = 0.871 (beta = -0.138 per SD), p=1.1e-30. Quartile analysis: response 20.4% (q0) vs 15.6% (q3 highest). Strong negative effect.
feature_024 OR per SD = 0.910 (beta = -0.094), p=2.4e-16. Quartiles: 20.7% vs 16.6% top quartile. Strong negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_022 (continuous, plausibly a marrow blast %, BM cellularity, or LDH-like lab) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher feature_027 (continuous, range 0.03-300, possibly beta-2-microglobulin) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Higher feature_029 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5, plausibly serum albumin) is associated with HIGHER objective_response rate (good nutrition predicts response).

analyses.result_summary: feature_022 OR per SD = 0.955, beta=-0.046, p=1.9e-4. Significant negative effect.
feature_027 OR per SD = 0.943, beta=-0.059, p=6.1e-6. Significant negative effect.
feature_029 OR per SD = 1.061, beta=+0.059, p=2.9e-7. Significant POSITIVE effect: higher feature_029 predicts higher response, consistent with an albumin-like marker of fitness.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous features feature_028 (hemoglobin-range), feature_034 (LDH/platelet-range), feature_007, feature_031, feature_013, feature_033 (bilirubin-range), feature_003 (creatinine-range), feature_035, feature_023 (sodium-range), feature_002 (potassium-range), feature_017 (calcium-range), and feature_012 do NOT show statistically meaningful univariate associations with objective_response.

analyses.code: for c in [feature_028, feature_034, feature_007, feature_031, feature_013, feature_033, feature_003, feature_035, feature_023, feature_002, feature_017, feature_012]: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(standardize(df[c]))).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All p-values > 0.05 except feature_007 (p=0.051). OR-per-SD all between 0.98 and 1.02. The 12 listed continuous labs/vitals show no main effect on response. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_001=1 have higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_001=0.
Patients with feature_014=1 have higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_014=0.
Patients with feature_021=1 have higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_021=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_001=1: response 22.10% (n=20056); feature_001=0: response 16.51% (n=29944). Difference +5.59 pp, log-OR=+0.36, chi2 p<1e-50.
feature_014=1: response 23.46% (n=15014); feature_014=0: response 16.73% (n=34986). Difference +6.74 pp, log-OR=+0.42, p=5.8e-70.
feature_021=1: response 21.96% (n=20020); feature_021=0: response 16.61% (n=29980). Difference +5.35 pp, log-OR=+0.35, p<1e-45.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_026=1 have lower objective_response rate.
Patients with feature_009=1 have lower objective_response rate.

The remaining binary features (feature_005, feature_010, feature_015, feature_016, feature_018, feature_019, feature_025, feature_006, feature_008, feature_030, feature_032) do NOT have significant univariate associations with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_026=1: 16.81% response (n=7462); feature_026=0: 19.09% (n=42538). Difference -2.29 pp, log-OR=-0.16, p=2.2e-7. Significant negative effect.
feature_009=1: 16.57% response (n=4985); feature_009=0: 18.99% (n=45015). Difference -2.42 pp, log-OR=-0.17, p=2.0e-5. Significant negative effect.

analyses.code: for each in [feature_005, feature_010, feature_015, feature_016, feature_018, feature_019, feature_025, feature_006, feature_008, feature_030, feature_032]: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df.objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: All 11 univariate chi-square tests have p>0.05; absolute response-rate differences range -0.7 pp to +0.4 pp. None show a meaningful main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a logistic regression with all 35 features as main effects, the significant predictors of objective_response will be: feature_004 (-), feature_020 (-), feature_024 (-), feature_022 (-), feature_027 (-), feature_029 (+), feature_001 (+), feature_014 (+), feature_021 (+), feature_026 (-), and feature_009 (-).

analyses.result_summary: All 11 predicted features are significant with the predicted signs (all p<0.001 except feature_022, p<0.001). Strongest log-ORs: feature_014 +0.44, feature_001 +0.37, feature_021 +0.36, feature_004 -0.36, feature_026 -0.17, feature_009 -0.19. Other features were non-significant. Hypothesis fully supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 and feature_021 interact: the joint subgroup feature_001=1 & feature_021=1 has substantially higher objective_response rate than would be expected from main effects (positive multiplicative interaction).
feature_014 and feature_001 interact in the same way (positive interaction beyond additive main effects).
feature_014 and feature_021 interact in the same way (positive interaction beyond additive main effects).

analyses.result_summary: Joint table: f001=0&f021=0 -> 17.0%; f001=1&f021=0 -> 16.4%; f001=0&f021=1 -> 16.2%; f001=1&f021=1 -> 30.8%. The +14 pp jump in the f001=1 & f021=1 cell vs ~16% in the other three cells indicates a strong positive interaction. Logistic with f001*f021 interaction term: interaction p<1e-60.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three-way interaction feature_014=1 & feature_001=1 & feature_021=1 produces a much higher objective_response rate (>50%) than any of the seven other cells of the 2x2x2 table.

analyses.result_summary: Eight-cell table — seven non-triple cells have response rates between 15.7% and 17.2%; the triple-positive cell f014=1 & f001=1 & f021=1 (n=2401) has response 63.31%. The three-way interaction term is highly significant in a saturated 2x2x2 logistic model: chi2=425.7 on 1 df, p=0. Triple-positive vs all-others: log-OR=+2.17 (OR=8.73), absolute response difference +46.8 pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treating feature_014 as a candidate treatment, its absolute treatment effect (response rate when feature_014=1 vs 0) is essentially zero in patients without the f001=1 & f021=1 biomarker pair, but is approximately +46 pp within the f001=1 & f021=1 biomarker-positive subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_014 by (f001,f021) stratum: (0,0) diff=-1.2 pp p=0.05; (1,0) diff=-0.5 pp p=0.52; (0,1) diff=-0.5 pp p=0.48; (1,1) diff=+46.4 pp p<1e-300. Effect is concentrated entirely in the biomarker-positive (f001=1 & f021=1) subgroup. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: By symmetry, defining either feature_001 or feature_021 as the candidate 'treatment' produces the same picture: an effect concentrated only in the cell where the other two are =1. Therefore from the data alone the treatment role cannot be uniquely assigned among (feature_014, feature_001, feature_021).

analyses.code: for treatment in [feature_014, feature_001, feature_021]: subgroup analysis stratifying by other two
analyses.result_summary: Symmetry confirmed. Treatment-effect of feature_001: (f014=1,f021=1) +47.6 pp, all other three cells -0.7/0/+0.7 pp. Treatment-effect of feature_021: (f001=1,f014=1) +47.4 pp, all other three cells -1/-0.3/+0.5 pp. The 3-way pattern is symmetric. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup (n=2401, response 63%), feature_026=1 dramatically reduces objective_response (from 71% to 18%), acting as a resistance marker that abolishes the triple-positive benefit.
Within the triple-positive subgroup, feature_009=1 also dramatically reduces objective_response (from 69% to 19%), acting as a second resistance marker.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive: feature_026=1 (n=376) response 18.4%; feature_026=0 (n=2025) response 71.7%. Difference -53.3 pp, chi2 p=7.6e-86. feature_026 acts as a resistance marker selectively in this responder subgroup.
Within triple-positive: feature_009=1 (n=276) response 18.8%; feature_009=0 (n=2125) response 69.1%. Difference -50.2 pp, chi2 p=3.3e-59. Second resistance marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The patient subgroup defined by feature_014=1 AND feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 has objective_response rate >75%, vastly higher than the cohort baseline of ~17%. This is the maximally-responsive subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=1790, objective_response = 0.7860; complement n=48210, response 0.1653. Difference +62.1 pp, log-OR=+2.92 (OR=18.6), p=0. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the triple-positive subgroup, feature_026 and feature_009 do NOT meaningfully alter objective_response, showing they only act as resistance markers conditional on the f014/f001/f021-defined responder subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: In non-triple-positive subgroup (n=47599): f026=0,f009=0 16.45%; f026=0,f009=1 16.55%; f026=1,f009=0 16.83%; f026=1,f009=1 15.82%. Range only 1 pp. f026 and f009 have no meaningful effect outside the triple-positive subgroup. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within biomarker-positive (f001=1 & f021=1) and resistance-negative (f026=0 & f009=0) patients, the treatment-effect of feature_014 is approximately +62 percentage points (response 78.6% with f014=1 vs 16.6% with f014=0), the largest treatment effect identifiable in the cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Within biomarker+ resistance- subgroup: f014=1 (n=1790) response 78.6%; f014=0 (n=4288) response 16.6%. Treatment effect = +62.0 pp, chi2 p=0. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within biomarker-positive patients (f001=1 & f021=1), patients with either resistance marker (f026=1 OR f009=1) have ZERO treatment effect of feature_014 (treated and untreated both ~17-19% response).

analyses.result_summary: Biomarker+, f026=1: f014=1 18.4% (n=376) vs f014=0 18.6% (n=830), diff -0.2 pp, p=0.997. Biomarker+, f009=1: f014=1 18.8% (n=276) vs f014=0 17.1% (n=596), diff +1.7 pp, p=0.60. Either resistance marker eliminates the treatment benefit entirely. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-positive resistance-negative responder subgroup, the magnitude of the f014 treatment effect declines monotonically with feature_004 level: the absolute response on treatment is 86%/77%/67% at f004 levels 0/1/2 respectively (vs ~20%/15%/11% off treatment).

analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup: (f014=0,f004=0) 21.6%; (f014=0,f004=1) 14.9%; (f014=0,f004=2) 10.7%; (f014=1,f004=0) 85.9% (n=617); (f014=1,f004=1) 77.2% (n=902); (f014=1,f004=2) 66.8% (n=271). Treatment effects by f004: +64.3 / +62.3 / +56.1 pp. Saturated logistic interaction term (f014 x f004) p=0.24 — on the log-OR scale the interaction is not significant (constant odds-ratio); on the absolute scale lower-risk patients have larger benefit. f004 effect within treated patients chi2 p=4.3e-10.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The f014 treatment effect within the responder subgroup does NOT vary materially with age (feature_011): older patients respond similarly to younger patients.

The f014 treatment effect (absolute response rate difference) is reasonably stable across quartiles of the strongest continuous predictors (feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_027): all between +5 and +8 pp, with no quartile showing a strikingly larger or smaller benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Within fully-responder subgroup (n=1790, f014=1): age q0 (median 55.7 y) response 79.8%; q1 (64.5y) 81.7%; q2 (71.4y) 77.4%; q3 (80.4y) 75.6%. Slight downward trend with age but no large modifier; absolute response remains >75% across all ages.

analyses.code: for c in [feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, feature_011]: by quartile, compute treatment-effect of f014 (cohort-wide)
analyses.result_summary: Cohort-wide f014 treatment-effect (absolute) by quartiles: feature_020 +6.3/+7.2/+7.3; feature_024 +7.0/+6.6/+5.2/+8.1; feature_022 +7.1/+6.4/+6.3/+7.2; feature_027 +8.2/+6.9/+5.9/+6.1; feature_011 +7.2/+7.5/+5.9/+6.3 pp. Treatment-effect varies modestly (5-8 pp) and is stable across continuous-feature strata. Hypothesis supported — these are not material effect modifiers beyond the f001/f021/f026/f009 subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup, no binary feature OTHER than feature_026 and feature_009 substantially modifies the objective_response rate. In particular, sex (feature_010), feature_005, feature_015, feature_016, feature_018, feature_019, feature_025, feature_006, feature_008, feature_030, feature_032 do not show significant within-triple effect.

analyses.code: sub = df[triple]; for c in remaining binary cols: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(sub[c], sub.objective_response))
analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive (n=2401), tested 11 other binary features: all chi2 p-values > 0.13 except f026 and f009. Largest absolute response-rate differences: feature_018 +3.7 pp (p=0.22); feature_015 -4.8 pp (p=0.14); all others under 5 pp with p>0.27. Hypothesis supported — the only effect modifiers within the responder subgroup are f026 and f009.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the f014=1 responder-defined subgroup (biomarker+, resistance-), feature_004 (ordinal), feature_020, and feature_022 are the only continuous/ordinal predictors that meaningfully reduce objective_response.

analyses.code: sub2 = df[(f001=1)&(f021=1)&(f026=0)&(f009=0)&(f014=1)]; for each continuous: sm.Logit(y_sub, sm.add_constant(z))
analyses.result_summary: Within fully-responsive subgroup (n=1790): feature_004 beta=-0.38 p=9.6e-11 (strongest); feature_020 beta=-0.20 per SD p=4.3e-4; feature_022 beta=-0.09 p=0.08 borderline; feature_029 beta=+0.10 p=0.10 borderline; all other 14 continuous features p>0.13. The same predictors that affect baseline response also affect within-responder response, but with smaller magnitudes.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive scan of all C(16,3)=560 three-way binary feature combinations identifies (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) as the UNIQUE triple producing high (>45%) objective_response rate in a substantial subgroup (n>=200). No other triple matches this signature.

analyses.code: for triples (a,b,c) in itertools.combinations(binary_cols, 3): mask=(df[a]==1)&(df[b]==1)&(df[c]==1); if mask.sum()>200 and df.loc[mask,'objective_response'].mean()>0.45: keep
analyses.result_summary: Only one triple meets the criterion: (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021), n=2401, response 0.6331. No other triple of binary features comes close. The treatment-biomarker matching pattern is unique. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The triple-positive subgroup is balanced with the rest of the cohort on baseline characteristics — age, sex, the lab values, feature_004, etc. — indicating the high response is NOT due to the responder subgroup being healthier or younger; it is a true treatment-by-biomarker interaction.

analyses.code: for c in all features: compare mean(triple) vs mean(rest)
analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive vs non-triple: mean age 67.81 vs 68.00 (diff -0.2 yr); proportion feature_010=1: 0.444 vs 0.448; feature_004 mean 0.802 vs 0.797; feature_020 3.91 vs 3.85; feature_024 59.9 vs 60.0; feature_022 20.4 vs 20.2; feature_028 12.5 vs 12.5; feature_034 222 vs 224. All differences small (<2% of standard deviation). The triple-positive group is essentially exchangeable with the rest on baseline characteristics — confirming this is a treatment effect, not selection bias. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the (feature_001=1 & feature_021=1) biomarker-positive subgroup, the treatment effect of feature_014 is small and slightly NEGATIVE (about -1 pp), suggesting feature_014 is NOT beneficial in biomarker-negative patients and may be marginally harmful or neutral.

analyses.code: for biomarker stratum: estimate response on f014=1 vs f014=0
analyses.result_summary: Biomarker-negative strata: f001=0,f021=0 stratum, f014 effect -1.2 pp (p=0.05); f001=0,f021=1 stratum, -0.5 pp (p=0.48); f001=1,f021=0 stratum, -0.5 pp (p=0.52). The pooled biomarker-negative treatment effect is approximately -0.7 pp, marginally non-zero. Treatment is not helpful (and possibly slightly harmful) outside the responder subgroup. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic model that includes (a) the main effects of f014, f001, f021, f026, f009; (b) the f001*f021 biomarker term; (c) the (1-f026)*(1-f009)*f001*f021 'biomarker without resistance' term; and (d) the f014 x (1-f026)*(1-f009)*f001*f021 interaction term will capture essentially all of the response signal — with the f014 treatment effect concentrated in the (b)x(no resistance) intersection.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(X)).fit() with the 8 specified predictors
analyses.result_summary: Final saturated model: const=-1.58 (baseline log-odds ~0.17 prob); f014=-0.054 (n.s.), f001=-0.037 (n.s.), f021=-0.056 (n.s.), f026=+0.009 (n.s.), f009=-0.012 (n.s.); biomarker (f001*f021) = +0.18 p=0.017; biomarker_no_resist = -0.116 p=0.14; f014_x_biomarker_no_resist = +2.97 (OR=19.5) p<1e-300. The main treatment effect is concentrated entirely in the biomarker-positive resistance-negative subgroup interaction term. Hypothesis fully supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Treatment-feature feature_014 produces a large positive effect on objective_response (+62 percentage points absolute, OR ~19) ONLY in patients meeting the joint criteria feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0. In patients failing any of these four criteria, feature_014 has no meaningful effect on objective_response. Within the responder subgroup, f014=1 yields ~78.6% response vs f014=0 yields ~16.6% response (n=1790 treated, n=4288 untreated; difference p~0). Higher feature_004 (ordinal risk) attenuates the absolute (but not relative-OR) magnitude of benefit.

FINAL BASELINE-PROGNOSTIC HYPOTHESIS: Independent of treatment, the strongest negative prognostic factors for objective_response are feature_004 (ordinal, OR 0.69 per level), feature_020 (continuous, OR 0.87 per SD), feature_024 (continuous, OR 0.91 per SD), feature_027 (OR 0.94), feature_022 (OR 0.96), and the binary resistance markers feature_026 (OR 0.85) and feature_009 (OR 0.83). The strongest positive prognostic factor besides treatment is feature_029 (continuous, OR 1.06 per SD). Age (feature_011) and sex (feature_010) have no measurable effect.

analyses.code: Final 5-feature subgroup definition validated against the 50000 patient cohort.
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect of feature_014 in fully-defined responder subgroup [feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0]: response 78.60% (f014=1, n=1790) vs 16.63% (f014=0, n=4288); absolute difference +61.97 pp; log-OR +2.92 (OR=18.55); p~0. Hypothesis fully supported.

analyses.code: Multivariable logistic regression of all features on objective_response.
analyses.result_summary: All predicted prognostic effects are confirmed in the multivariable model with the predicted directions (all p<0.001 for the seven negative and one positive predictor). Age and sex remain non-significant. Hypothesis supported.
